mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing activity, which leads to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. This relationship is always active, reflecting the leading indicator property of INDPRO.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of INDPRO changes on 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or if the correlation becomes consistently negative over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 8%, it signals a severely weakened labor market and heightened recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter. The threshold condition activates only when UNRATE exceeds this level.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return during UNRATE > 8% periods is non-negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in unemployment and decrease in housing starts signals a deepening recession. When UNRATE rises more than 0.5pp over 3 months and HOUST falls more than 5% over the same period, the S&P 500 tends to decline over the following quarter due to collapsing demand and consumer confidence.
failure_condition: If the joint signal produces positive median 60-day returns more than 40% of the time, or if the interaction term in a regression (UNRATE * HOUST change) is not statistically significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the federal funds rate is extremely low (below 0.5%), further declines signal exceptionally accommodative policy but also deflation risk and desperation. Within this regime, a decreasing FEDFUNDS predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as markets interpret it as a sign of crisis.
failure_condition: If the correlation between weekly changes in FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns in the low-rate regime is positive or not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, a key driver of corporate profits and a positive signal for S&P 500 returns over the following week. The signal is always active because consumer demand is a persistent fundamental.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return predictability of RSAFS monthly changes is not significant at the 5% level in a time-series regression over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 500 thousand units, it signals a contraction in the housing market and broader economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. The threshold is crossed only during housing downturns.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return during HOUST < 500 periods is not negative on average, or if the signal fails to generate a Sharpe ratio worse than -0.5.

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) increase month-over-month, the economy is in a confirmed expansion phase. This joint positive signal predicts rising S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings and employment momentum reinforce each other.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return in months with both PAYEMS and INDPRO rising is not significantly greater than the unconditional average (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment (UNRATE above 8%), positive surprises in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) are interpreted as early signs of labor market recovery. This leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as markets price in an improving economy.
failure_condition: If the information coefficient between monthly PAYEMS changes and 60-day forward returns in high-unemployment months is below 0.05 or non-positive over any 5-year period.
